content: Just as the language education definition of reflexion frames it as a metacognitive step that turns passive vocabulary input into active usable linguistic knowledge, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to mirror this structured educational reflexion workflow, rather than relying on unguided ad-hoc self-correction. This means aligning LLM self-reflection stages to the same scaffolded reflexion used to help high school students consolidate problem-solving skills: first encoding output errors, then reflecting on mismatches between intended and actual results, then integrating corrected patterns into long-term usable memory.
